# Structure identifiers

Namespace: `structure`

## What this supports

Chemlake resolves Structure identifiers queries from local lake data and returns ranked compound matches. Request-time resolution is offline; this route never calls an upstream service.

## Accepted inputs

`InChI, InChIKey, SMILES`

## Local artifact requirements

- `compound_identity.jsonl/parquet`

The artifact must contain values that can be normalized into this namespace and joined to a Chemlake compound identity.

## Resolver route

- Route: **Structure index**
- Behavior: Exact InChI, InChIKey, and SMILES fields from local identity artifacts.
- Ranking: candidates are merged with other matching routes, then scored with ambiguity-aware confidence components when `--confidence` is requested.

## Outputs

inchi, inchikey, smiles, formula, mass, SDF/molfile when present.

When `--to all` is requested, this route can also return linked names, synonyms, structures, source xrefs, spectra, evidence summaries, usage/commonness, provenance, and freshness when those fields are available locally.

## CLI examples

```bash
chemlake --data-dir ./lake resolve --input queries.txt --from structure --to all --confidence
printf '%s
' 'InChI' | chemlake --data-dir ./lake resolve --input - --to all --confidence
```

## API example

```json
{
  "queries": ["InChI"],
  "from": "structure",
  "to": "all",
  "confidence": true
}
```

## Notes

No additional caveats beyond local artifact availability.

## Updating this README
